canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, MAMO is consistently described as a benchmark for evaluating mathematical modeling. The most common definition characterizes it as a tool for benchmarking modeling that requires the use of code solvers, with a specific focus on linear programming problems. A complementary framing, present in one paper, defines MAMO by its two optimization datasets, EasyLP and ComplexLP, which are used to evaluate LLM performance. In practice, MAMO is used to assess fine-tuned models, often alongside other benchmarks like NL4OPT, IndustryOR, and OptiBench. Models are reported to achieve "state-of-the-art performance" on MAMO, positioning it as an established instrument for measuring modeling capabilities.
papers: LLMOPT: Learning to Define and Solve General Optimization Problems from Scratch
OptiBench Meets ReSocratic: Measure and Improve LLMs for Optimization Modeling
EquivaMap: Leveraging LLMs for Automatic Equivalence Checking of Optimization Formulations
OptMATH: A Scalable Bidirectional Data Synthesis Framework for Optimization Modeling

definitions.text: A benchmark for mathematical modeling that requires the use of code solvers, focusing on linear programming problems.
definitions.paper: OptiBench Meets ReSocratic: Measure and Improve LLMs for Optimization Modeling

definitions.text: A benchmark for mathematical modeling with two optimization datasets, EasyLP and ComplexLP, used to evaluate LLM performance.
definitions.paper: LLMOPT: Learning to Define and Solve General Optimization Problems from Scratch
